docs.title: User Guide
docs.subtitle: Everything you need to get started

why.title: Why Mathakine?
why.intro: Mathakine is designed so everyone progresses at their own pace, without pressure or cognitive overload. It's the ideal tool to regain self-confidence.
why.safeDesc: No punitive grades, no stressful timers. The "Zero Noise" interface helps focus without assaulting the eyes.
why.paceDesc: No more exercises that are too hard or too easy. The algorithm analyzes your answers and adapts to your real level in real-time.
why.progressDesc: Active streaks, daily challenges, and collectible badges: everything is built to reward consistency and celebrate every small step.

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.step1Desc: Sign up with your email. Don't forget to verify it to unlock all features (badges, dashboard).
getStarted.step2Desc: Take a short diagnostic test (10 questions) so Mathakine can understand your profile and calibrate your first exercises.
getStarted.step3Desc: On your dashboard, use the quick start buttons to instantly launch the exercise or challenge recommended for you, or start a custom session with your preferred filters.

using.title: At the heart of learning
using.exercisesDesc: Addition, fractions, geometry... Artificial intelligence generates infinite exercises. The algorithm adjusts difficulty for you—from discovery level through advanced—using multiple-choice or free input based on your needs.
using.challengesDesc: Sequences, visual patterns, riddles, probability... Train your critical thinking. Some challenges are AI-generated to provide infinite replayability.
using.dashboardDesc: Find 3 daily goals to complete to earn bonus XP and keep your streak alive. Track your accuracy by category and export your log as a PDF.
using.badgesDesc: Earn XP with every success to level up. Unlock dozens of unique badges illustrating your mathematical progress.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.intro: Mathakine offers options to suit your needs (contrast, text, animations, Focus TSA/ADHD mode).
accessibility.whereDesc: Settings or accessibility bar: Alt+C (contrast), Alt+T (larger text), Alt+M (reduced motion), Alt+D (dyslexia), Alt+F (Focus TSA/ADHD).

retention.title: Tips to stay motivated
retention.shortDesc: 5 to 15 minutes a day is already great. Consistency matters more than quantity.
retention.celebrateDesc: Every badge, every completed exercise is a win. Take time to enjoy them.
retention.chooseDesc: You decide what to do. Math becomes easier when you have fun.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Is the site adapted for children with ASD/ADHD/DYS?
faq.a1: Yes. Mathakine offers a "Focus" mode, reduces animations, includes a dyslexia filter, and removes visual noise (Glassmorphism).
faq.q2: How does the initial evaluation work?
faq.a2: It starts automatically to target your level. 10 questions are enough to set up your space and adapt the difficulty.
faq.a3: Yes! Mathakine's Artificial Intelligence modifies the difficulty based on your answers. If you struggle, it may even switch to multiple-choice answers.
faq.q4: Where can I see my progress?
faq.a4: In the "Dashboard" menu. You'll find your daily challenges, global accuracy, current streaks, and all your badges.
faq.a5: Every day, Mathakine generates 3 personalized goals to encourage you to practice across different categories. They reward you with bonus XP.
faq.q6: Do I have to choose the difficulty manually?
faq.a6: No, it's optional. The algorithm adjusts difficulty in the background based on your results and initial assessment. If no data is available, it uses the "Preferred age group" (6-8 years, 9-11 years, etc.) set in your Settings as a starting point.
faq.q7: The interface is too cluttered for me.
faq.a7: Open Settings (or press Alt+F) to enable Focus Mode. You can also reduce animations (Alt+M) and change the theme (Dune, Forest...).
faq.a8: No. The system rewards accumulated experience (XP). The goal is to practice without fear of failure and to learn from detailed explanations (LaTeX).
faq.a9: Yes! Install the Progressive Web App (PWA) from your browser to use it without the internet (mode restricted to exercises).
faq.q10: An exercise or challenge seems incorrect?
faq.a10: Use the report button (red flag) at the bottom of each exercise page. We will correct the error as quickly as possible.

about.title: About Mathakine
about.subtitle: A platform born from a family story

story.title: A story born from necessity
story.p1: Mathakine was not born in the offices of a big Tech company, nor from a desire to revolutionize global education. Mathakine was born from a very personal need: that of a father seeking to help his autistic son make peace with mathematics.
story.p2: When faced with traditional learning platforms, one thing became clear: they are often ill-suited to neuroatypical children (ASD, ADHD, DYS). The garish colours, cluttered interfaces, stressful timers and flashing red "WRONG" signs were not testing my son's calculation skills. They were measuring his ability to withstand <bold>sensory and cognitive overload</bold>.
story.p3: The energy he should have used to understand a fraction was wasted sorting through the "visual noise" on the screen.
story.p4: So I decided to build him a tailor-made space.

philosophy.title: The "Zero Noise" philosophy
philosophy.intro: To design Mathakine, I set aside superfluous animations to focus on what cognitive science teaches us: attention is a limited resource.

philosophy1.title: A calming environment
philosophy1.desc: The interface (based on Glassmorphism) gently frames the problem. Colours are designed not to assault the eye, with adaptive themes (Space, Dune, Dino) so the child can choose the universe in which they feel safe.

philosophy2.title: Absolute clarity
philosophy2.desc: Mathematics should be effortless to read. That's why every calculation, every fraction and every square root uses the scientific typographic standard (LaTeX). The eye glides over the explanation.

philosophy3.title: The right to make mistakes
philosophy3.desc: Mistakes are no longer punitive. They are a normal step that triggers a detailed step-by-step explanation, valuing effort rather than the immediate answer.

philosophy4.title: Gentle anchoring
philosophy4.desc: The spaced repetition algorithm (SM-2) brings concepts back at the right moment to consolidate memory without exhausting the student with useless block repetitions.

curb.title: The "Curb cut" effect
curb.p1: In urban planning, lowering kerbs was initially designed for wheelchair users. But very quickly, we realised it also helped parents with prams, travellers with suitcases and cyclists.
curb.p3: What was essential for my son — a distraction-free environment, structured learning, readable fonts — turned out to be an absolute comfort and a learning accelerator for all students, neurotypical and atypical alike, children and adults. That's why I decided to open this platform to the public.

transparency.title: A transparent approach
transparency.p1: I am not a professional developer. To build this platform, I used Artificial Intelligence as a "specialist worker", while keeping the hat of architect and attentive dad.
transparency.p2: Mathakine is the fruit of my evenings and weekends. The platform is now in Beta. There are probably still a few bugs or adjustments to be made. But it is made with heart, the standards of a parent, and a sincere desire to help.
transparency.p3: Your feedback, ideas and comments are welcome. Feel free to use the "Report an issue" button if you come across an error.
transparency.p4: Thank you for being part of this adventure.
transparency.signature: Created by Zyclope, Anakyn's dad — A dad who's almost a developer

form.name: Name
form.subject: Subject

feedback.intro: Incorrect exercise, incoherent challenge, or a bug?
feedback.desc: Use the report button (red flag) at the bottom right on any page to send us detailed feedback. Your reports are visible to the team and help us improve Mathakine.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How Mathakine handles your personal data

controller.title: Data controller
controller.desc: In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
controller.text: The data controller is Yannick Malgorn. For any questions regarding your personal data: webmaster@mathakine.fun

data.title: Data collected and purposes
data.intro: Mathakine collects only the data necessary for the service to operate.

account.title: User account
account.desc: Username, email address, password (hashed). Purpose: account creation, authentication, password recovery.

progress.title: Progress and activity
progress.desc: Exercise results, attempts, completed challenges, badges earned. Purpose: progress tracking, recommendation personalization, dashboard.

feedback.title: Reports
feedback.desc: Report messages (incorrect exercise, bug) with relevant page. Purpose: improving content and interface.

technical.title: Technical data
technical.desc: Technical logs, session identifiers, error traces (Sentry). Purpose: service operation, incident diagnostics.

retention.title: Retention period
retention.intro: Data is retained for the following periods, in line with the GDPR minimization principle.
retention.account: Account: until deletion by the user or erasure request.
retention.progress: Progress and activity: same duration as the account. Deleted when the account is deleted.
retention.feedback: Reports: 2 years after closure (or account deletion if associated).

rights.title: Your rights
rights.intro: You have the following rights regarding your data:
rights.access: Right of access: obtain a copy of your data.
rights.rectification: Right to rectification: correct inaccurate data.
rights.erasure: Right to erasure: request deletion of your data.
rights.portability: Right to portability: receive your data in a structured format.
rights.objection: Right to object: object to certain processing.
rights.complaint: Right to lodge a complaint: contact your data protection authority if you believe the processing is non-compliant.

subcontractors.title: Subcontractors
subcontractors.intro: Certain service providers process data on behalf of Mathakine.
subcontractors.note: Each subcontractor is contractually bound to comply with the GDPR. Standard policies, not negotiated.
subcontractors.render: Render: application and database hosting (USA, standard contractual clauses).
subcontractors.sentry: Sentry: error monitoring and technical diagnostics (minimal traces).
subcontractors.email: SendGrid or SMTP: sending verification and password reset emails.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.intro: Mathakine uses strictly necessary cookies for the service to function.
cookies.essential: Authentication cookies (access_token, refresh_token): maintaining the logged-in session. CSRF cookie: protection against cross-site attacks. These cookies are essential; refusing them prevents use of the service.
cookies.noMarketing: No advertising cookies or third-party trackers.

changelog.title: What's new
changelog.subtitle: Latest improvements to Mathakine, explained simply.
changelog.scopeNote: This page lists visible product releases. Internal engineering milestones stay in the root project changelog.
changelog.alphaNote: Mathakine remains in alpha: the product is stable in use, but surfaces and flows are still evolving.

items: My space is now the real learner home, with a calmer structure: quick actions, reviews, challenges, and progress in one clear flow.
Visible user roles are now clear and business-oriented: Learner, Teacher, Moderator, and Admin.
The learner/adult boundary is more robust: /home-learner, /dashboard, and /admin are filtered before render, while the learner still keeps discreet secondary access to the dashboard.
The theme system now exposes 8 visible themes, with Aurora replacing the old Peach theme and a full Unicorn theme.
Theme changes and light/dark switching are smoother, without the previous layered color-jank effect.
The Exercises page is easier to scan: filters are calmer, cards are lighter, and keyboard access is stronger.
The Challenges page is now visually aligned with Exercises, with a cleaner first-contact flow for starting without hesitation.
The Leaderboard and several navigation surfaces received visible polish and accessibility cleanup without changing the main journeys.
Spaced repetition is now visible and actionable from the dashboard, with a clear daily summary.
The Review now button opens the next due card directly, without creating a second parallel learning flow.
The review session reuses the exercise solver, then gently offers the next due card or a calm end-of-session state.
Hints and explanations stay hidden before you answer, then the explanation appears after submission to reinforce learning.
The dashboard has been made calmer and easier to scan around the review area, with less visual noise and clearer hierarchy.
Pedagogical difficulty is now more precise: the platform combines age band and mastery level to calibrate exercises and challenges more accurately.
The AI challenge generator can now use your profile automatically in the recommended mode, while still letting you choose a specific age when you want to.
Account progression is now clearer: numeric level is shown separately from the public progression rank across the profile, dashboard, badges, and leaderboard.
The account progression curve is less inflationary, so levels and ranks grow in a more durable way than with the old flat model.
The main visible references to the old licensed narrative have been removed from the most exposed product and admin surfaces.
You earn points when you complete a logic challenge, with clearer feedback in the interface.
The dashboard brings exercises and challenges together: one theme chart and a clearer challenges section.
Your progress timeline can include challenges alongside exercises.
Challenge stats and active lists now follow the same rules everywhere for more consistent results.
Sort order on the Exercises and Challenges pages (random or most recent) is kept after you refresh the browser.
You now earn account points when you succeed on a standard exercise, consistent with other rewards.
Random challenge lists stay fast even when there is a lot of content.
Email-related logs are safer for privacy: full addresses are no longer printed in technical logs.
More reliable point grants on the server under parallel load (no everyday change to how you play).
The chat now understands exercise and challenge requests more reliably and avoids off-topic responses, such as generating an image when you simply ask for an exercise.
AI exercise and challenge generation is now more reliable, with stricter validation, fewer false successes, and clearer end-of-generation states.
After a successful generation, you can reopen the newly created exercise or challenge directly from the widget.
Overall AI stability has been reinforced before the next alpha steps: chat, exercises, challenges, and runtime monitoring are now better aligned.
Dashboard recommendations are now more stable from one refresh to the next.
Exercise and challenge suggestions now use clearer, better translated reason labels in the interface.
Discovery of new exercise types now follows a more consistent selection path with the rest of the recommendation engine.
This is primarily a product and quality release: the recommendation engine is stronger, without pretending to be a learned AI system.
Changing or resetting your password now revokes older sessions, so an older tab must sign in again.
Account, profile, and session management are now more reliable on the backend.
Personal data export is now properly wired and covered by an API test.
This is mainly a technical release focused on reliability across exercises, authentication, and the user area.
New progress timeline chart in the dashboard to follow your results over 7 or 30 days.
Clearer exercise volume chart in the Progress tab.
New interleaved practice session to vary exercises automatically inside one run.
Adaptive exercise generation is now better calibrated when adaptive mode is enabled.
Home page refreshed with a stronger hero, clearer tagline, and easier access to the Assistant from navigation.
Exercises and Challenges pages now use more compact filters and generator controls.
Exercise modal readability improved, with a clearer Validate action.
Additional stability and polish fixes across existing flows.
New daily challenges with 3 goals to keep practice regular.
Dashboard reorganized with a simpler overview and a dedicated My Profile tab.
Daily challenges and current streak now sit side by side for a clearer overview.
Challenge visuals improved, especially for XP badges and labels.
Better skill tracking for more personalized difficulty.
Clearer support during difficult exercises.
New progress widgets added to the dashboard.
Answer modes are now better aligned with the learner's measured level.
Adaptive placement test now available with 10 questions across 4 operations.
The test can be retaken at any time from Settings.
Recommendations now use the level actually measured by the test.
Exercise, challenge, and diagnostic pages now display correctly across all themes.
Exercise explanations no longer show the technical prefix [TEST-ZAXXON].
Duplicate answer choices were fixed in some division exercises.
The exercise page was fully redesigned with a more immersive centered panel.
Answer tiles now feel clearer and more responsive visually.
The Validate button is better staged and activates more clearly.
Success states feel more rewarding and explanations read more like knowledge cards.
The challenge page was also redesigned with a better integrated answer area.
The Hint button now stands out more clearly as optional help.
Clicking outside the exercise window now closes it correctly.
The Mathakine logo now appears in the top navigation.
The home page shows the illustrated logo instead of a plain text title.
The app icon now uses the official logo in the browser and on mobile install.
The badges page now includes encouragement based on your collection progress.
A confetti burst appears the first time you return after unlocking a new badge.
A Within reach panel highlights the badges closest to being unlocked.
Badge cards now feel more premium with stronger visual emphasis on earned badges.
Locked badges display a lock and favorites now use a heart icon.
Dashboard charts now follow your selected theme.
Home page feature cards now include a stronger hover depth effect.
Chart and visualization animations better respect accessibility preferences.
Badge icons now include descriptive text for screen readers.
Input fields are more consistent across the app.
Faster access to the first exercise after sign-up, with a grace period before email verification is enforced.
Onboarding now captures class, age group, goal, and rhythm to personalize recommendations.
Dashboard now offers a guided path with An exercise / A challenge calls to action.
EdTech analytics now track dashboard views, Quick Start clicks, and first attempts.
Profile and settings remain accessible for unverified users.
New About, Contact, and Privacy Policy pages.
New report button to flag a wrong exercise, inconsistent challenge, or bug.
Leaderboard can now be filtered by age group.
Dashboard recommendations and session metrics are more useful.
Chatbot and footer display are now more consistent.
Random order and hide-completed options for exercises and challenges.
Badges redesigned with rarity display, dedicated tabs, and favorites pinning.
Challenges improved with better answer handling and new puzzle types.
New themes: Dune, Forest, Light, and Dinosaurs.
User leaderboard.
Export your data as PDF or Excel.
Math assistant to help with exercises.
Accessibility options for contrast, text size, animations, and Focus mode.
Adaptive exercises for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
Varied logic challenges with riddles, chess, and puzzles.
Registration and login with email verification.
Dashboard with progress tracking.
Achievement badges.
Visual themes to customize the interface.
Backend consolidation on challenge/admin/badge with cleaner HTTP boundaries and explicit application facades.
Dedicated API proofs added for admin content endpoints and public/user badge endpoints in scope.
Auth/admin fixtures and non-deterministic challenge tests stabilized on the current tree.
Primarily a technical release: hardening, documentation alignment, and backend iteration closure.

aiMonitoring.title: AI monitoring
aiMonitoring.description: Estimated costs and runtime quality: authenticated rate-limited assistant chat, AI exercises, AI challenges. Process data has bounded retention; persisted harness at the bottom of the page.
aiMonitoring.errorLoading: Failed to load. Check your admin permissions.
aiMonitoring.limitsChat: The help chat is <s>authenticated</s> (JWT + cookies on <c>/api/chat</c> and the Next proxy) and <s>rate-limited</s> — its weight appears in the workloads below.
aiMonitoring.limitsCosts: USD costs are <s>estimates</s> (indicative grids), not accounting truth.
aiMonitoring.fallbackRetention: Runtime aggregates: in-process memory purged beyond the retention window.
aiMonitoring.fallbackMetrics: For frozen runs, see the persisted harness section below.
aiMonitoring.detailByKeyDesc: Fine detail by internal key: challenge type, AI exercise type or chat segment.
aiMonitoring.byModelDesc: Actual runtime distribution of calls by model family.
aiMonitoring.noGenTitle: No AI generations recorded for this period.
aiMonitoring.noGenHint: Data accumulates from chat calls, AI exercises and AI challenges.
aiMonitoring.harnessCardDesc: Offline/live mode, target, corpus and counters at run time. Artifact paths if saved server-side.
aiMonitoring.harnessEmpty: No harness run persisted in the database yet.
